title: Understanding a Rehabilitation Stay: What Happens There
summary: A rehab stay after a hospitalization has its own rhythm and goals, different from either the hospital or home. Here's what to expect.
category: Life Transitions I: The Hospital-to-Home Arc
journeyStage: Life Transitions I: The Hospital-to-Home Arc
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Understanding a Rehabilitation Stay: What Happens There | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: A rehab stay after a hospitalization has its own rhythm and goals, different from either the hospital or home. Here's what to expect.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Rehab focuses on regaining specific functional skills, with daily therapy sessions.
Length of stay depends on progress toward set goals, not a fixed calendar.
Insurance coverage rules (especially Medicare's) affect how long a stay is covered.

paragraphs: Unlike a hospital stay focused on medical stabilization, a rehab stay centers on structured, daily physical, occupational, and sometimes speech therapy aimed at specific functional goals — walking a certain distance, managing stairs, or handling daily tasks independently again.

Insurance coverage, especially Medicare's specific rules around skilled nursing and rehab stays, can be confusing and directly affects how long a stay is covered — it's worth asking the facility's social worker or discharge planner about this early, not near the end of the stay.

nextSteps: Ask what specific functional goals are being targeted.
Attend a therapy session if allowed, to understand the daily routine.
Ask about insurance coverage limits early in the stay, not at the end.
disclaimer: General overview — a facility's social worker or discharge planner can address the specific insurance and timeline questions for a given stay.

sources.name: Medicare.gov: Medicare Coverage of Skilled Nursing Facility Care
